data.title: Alliant Energy declares $0.5075 dividend
data.description:
• Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT) declared $0.5075/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.16%
  • Payable Aug. 15; for shareholders of record July 31; ex-div July 31.
  • See LNT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Abbott upgraded to Buy at Jefferies despite underwhelming outlook
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) was upgraded to Buy from Hold at Jefferies following its Q2 results and revised guidance, as analysts view the market reaction as “overly punitive” and see an attractive entry point amid transitory headwinds.

The company posted $11.1B in revenue, up 7% YoY, exceeding estimates by $80M, and delivered adjusted EPS of $1.26, in line with consensus.

While Q3 EPS guidance of $1.28–$1.32 came in slightly below the $1.33 consensus, analysts at Jefferies view these pressures, namely COVID testing declines and China’s VBP impact on diagnostics, as temporary. 

Jefferies expects growth to reaccelerate in 2026 as Abbott laps those headwinds and benefits from new product cycles across electrophysiology, diabetes, and structural heart.

The upgrade reflects confidence in the company’s execution, a strong pipeline, and improving margin outlook, Jefferies analysts added.

Jefferies has set its price target to $145, above the previous target of $143.

data.title: Humana has reportedly lost Medicare Star Ratings lawsuit
data.description:
Humana (NYSE:HUM) shares reached a session low in the morning hours on Friday after Bloomberg News reported that the managed care firm has lost a lawsuit that challenged its 2025 Medicare Star Ratings cut.

Shares of the Louisville, Kentucky-based health insurer plunged in October after the company, citing preliminary 2025 MA Star Ratings data, disclosed that Medicare Advantage members enrolled in its plans rated four stars and above in 2025 plunged to 25% compared to ~94% in 2024.

Humana (NYSE:HUM) contested the data, filing a lawsuit in federal court in the Northern District of Texas, stating that there may be errors in the calculation conducted by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS).

As part of the Star Ratings system, the CMS rewards plans with four or more stars with annual bonus payments, with insurers offering plans with higher ratings eligible to receive higher bonuses.

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: United's effective cost control, improving corporate demand to drive earnings growth - UBS
data.description:
United Airlines’ (NASDAQ:UAL) conservative EPS guidance for FY25 might have left Wall Street slightly unimpressed, but the $9 to $11 per share range convinced UBS’s Thomas Wadewitz that revenue at Newark Liberty International Airport is normalizing, and corporate travel is improving.

Accordingly, Wadewitz raises his price target for Buy-rated United (NASDAQ:UAL) by 11% to $114 and EPS estimates for Q4 by 17% to $2.90 and for FY26 by 11% to $14.25, supported by well controlled costs, improving demand, and recovery at Newark.

While the outlook towards Newark operations and improving corporate demand is supportive of improved profitability, the other half comes from United’s (NASDAQ:UAL) effective cost management, illustrated by an “impressive” CASM ex performance of only 2.2% year-over-year versus UBS’s +4.1% estimate. This was facilitated by strong network operations, even as the carrier took a charge for signing bonuses in the flight attendants’ new contract.

“Despite realizing the inflationary pressure from the [flight attendant] contract in the second half, United indicated they can continue to deliver well controlled CASM ex-fuel in H2 2025,” Wadewitz says, forecasting 2.5% CASM ex-fuel for the second half of this year on a 5% to 6% increase in capacity.

By comparison, Delta Air Lines (DAL), which was the first carrier to release Q2 results, reported a 2.7% increase in CASM ex-fuel for the second quarter on 4% capacity growth.

For the remainder of the airline industry, American Airlines Group (AAL) reports on July 24, expected to earn an adjusted profit of $0.77 per share on $14.3B in revenue.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) also releases second quarter results July 24, expected to earn an adjusted profit of $0.51 on $7.3B in revenue.

JetBlue (JBLU) reports on July 29, expected to show an adjusted loss of $0.33 per share on $2.29B in sales.

data.title: Chipotle is called best-in-class by BMO Capital amid economic slowdown concerns
data.description:
BMO Capital upgraded Chipotle (NYSE:CMG) to an Outperform rating on Friday after having the restaurant stock set at Market Perform.

Analyst Andrew Strelzik said the firm sees Chipotle (NYSE:CMG) as well positioned for accelerating comparable sales growth and an improving margin trajectory beginning in the second half of 2025. In addition, Strelzik and his team have a favorable view on Chipotle's (CMG) strong U.S.-heavy store growth that has room to accelerate towards 10% over time.

"We believe improvement in comp and margin trajectories combined with a widening gap to industry performance should warrant a higher multiple," highlighted Strelzik. "We believe value remains best-in-class, supporting relative traffic outperformance in the event of a macro slowdown," he added.

BMO Capital set a price target of $65 on Chipotle (CMG), which factors in a 45X P/E trading multiple, largely in line with CMG's historical average.

Shares of Chipotle (CMG) rose 0.4% in Friday morning trading to $53.73 vs. the 52-week range of $44.46 to $66.74.

data.title: Wall Street is mixed as record highs for S&P and Nasdaq are offset by Dow drag
data.description:
Just a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record territory, both indexes climbed to fresh highs once again on Friday, though weakness in American Express (AXP) has weighed on the Dow, leaving markets trading mixed.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is near flat while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) has slipped lower by 0.3%. At the same time, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has climbed up by 0.1% so far. 

As the S&P and Nasdaq top record highs yet again, Ryan Detrick of Carson Group stated “Number nine! Today was the 9th new all-time high of 2025 for the S&P 500. Since 1957 (when it became 500 stocks) today was ATH number 1,250. Congrats to those who followed their investment plan back in April.” 

Of the 11 sectors within the S&P 500, ten are trading in positive territory, led by strong gains in Utilities and Consumer Discretionary. Meanwhile, Communication Services lags behind, posting the steepest losses among the group. 

U.S. Treasury yields pushed lower as the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) has dipped 3 basis points to 3.87% and the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) has fallen 2 basis points to 4.42%. 

On the economic front, building permits and housing starts rose more than expected in June. Furthermore, the U.S. consumer sentiment gauge edged up to 61.8 in July from 60.7 in June, slightly exceeding the 61.4 consensus according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday. 

“It was a goldilocks week for economic data with most inflation readings coming in weaker than expected and everything else coming in better than expected. It wasn't quite an ‘immaculate’ week, but it was close,” Bespoke Investment said. 

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Netflix (NFLX) are lower by more than 5% after it reported its latest quarterly earning data after the bell on Thursday.

data.title: SEC's Atkins says he'll proceed carefully on potential 401(k) changes - report
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange will work closely with counterparts at the Labor Department in weighing possible changes to rules for retirement plans, SEC Chairman Paul Atkins chairman said in a broadcast interview on Friday.

"We have to do this in a smart way," he said on Bloomberg Television's Surveillance program.

The comment comes as the Trump administration is finalizing an executive order that could allow 401((k)) retirement plans to invest in private equity, as reported by Bloomberg earlier this week. No date has been set for the order's release, according to people familiar with the matter.

While demand for access to private equity is growing, Atkins noted that there are important differences with public market investments. A big one is liquidity. It's generally more complicated to cash out of private market investments than publicly traded securities.

Asked about whether crypto assets could be added to retirement plans, Atkins responded, "Disclosure is key, and that people need to know what they are getting into.... I look forward to whatever may come out from the president."

Since taking over as the SEC head, Atkins has eased the commission's scrutiny of crypto and has backed off of suits initiated by his predecessor Gary Gensler, who viewed most cryptocurrencies as securities. The SEC has also abandoned a crop of Biden-era proposals, including one that would have made it easier for shareholders to offer proposals at corporate annual meetings.

data.title: Crispr director George buys $51.5M of shares
data.description:
• Crispr Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP) has disclosed a $51.5 million stock purchase by board director Simeon George in an SEC filing.
  • Crispr surged as much as 21% on Friday, marking its biggest intraday gain since February, after. The stock is now up around 58.4% year-to-date.

data.title: Consumer sentiment hits highest mark in five months in July: UMich
data.description: The U.S. consumer sentiment gauge edged up to 61.8 in July from 60.7 in June, slightly exceeding the 61.4 consensus according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday.

data.title: Sable Offshore jumps after judge lays out Las Flores pipelines restart
data.description:
Sable Offshore (NYSE:SOC) +1.6% in early trading Friday following a 28.5% surge in the previous session after a California court decision partially granted preliminary injunctions related to the company's Las Flores pipelines, which allow the company to move forward with certain preparatory steps for restarting the pipelines, which have been shut down since a 2015 oil spill.

The court ruling allows Sable Offshore (NYSE:SOC) to proceed with obtaining necessary approvals and permits, but it prohibits the actual restart of the pipelines until 10 court days after the company provides a signed notice of compliance.

The Las Flores pipelines, connected to the Santa Ynez offshore platforms, have been subject to legal and environmental scrutiny since a rupture in 2015 led to their shutdown; Sable Offshore (NYSE:SOC) acquired the pipelines in 2024 and obtained state waivers, which petitioners said violated environmental laws due to the lack of updated reviews and public input.

The ruling means the "path is paved" for Sable Offshore (SOC) to receive a final affirmative ruling for its offshore pipeline to be restarted in the "very near future," Roth Capital analyst Leo Mariani said, maintaining his Buy rating on the stock with a $34 price target.
